subject: Chemistry
q:
$5.33$ g of $CrCl_3 \cdot 6H_2O$, which is a $1 : 3$ electrolyte, is dissolved in water and is passed through a cation exchanger. The chloride ions in the eluted solution, on treatment with $AgNO_3$ results in $8.61$ g of $AgCl$. The ratio of moles of complex reacted and moles of $AgCl$ formed is _______ $\times 10^{-2}$. (Nearest integer)
[Molar mass in g mol$^{-1}$ Cr : $52$, Ag : $108$, Cl : $35.5$, H : $1$, O : $16$]
question:
$5.33$ g of $CrCl_3 \cdot 6H_2O$, which is a $1 : 3$ electrolyte, is dissolved in water and is passed through a cation exchanger. The chloride ions in the eluted solution, on treatment with $AgNO_3$ results in $8.61$ g of $AgCl$. The ratio of moles of complex reacted and moles of $AgCl$ formed is _______ $\times 10^{-2}$. (Nearest integer)
[Molar mass in g mol$^{-1}$ Cr : $52$, Ag : $108$, Cl : $35.5$, H : $1$, O : $16$]
solution:
Molar mass of $CrCl_3 \cdot 6H_2O = 52 + 3 \times 35.5 + 6 \times 18 = 266.5 \text{ g mol}^{-1}$

Moles of complex reacted $= \dfrac{5.33}{266.5} = 0.02 \text{ mol}$

Since the complex is a $1:3$ electrolyte, it dissociates to give one complex cation and three chloride anions. Its formula is $[Cr(H_2O)_6]Cl_3$.

When passed through a cation exchanger, the complex cation is exchanged, and the chloride ions pass into the eluted solution.

Molar mass of $AgCl = 108 + 35.5 = 143.5 \text{ g mol}^{-1}$

Moles of $AgCl$ formed $= \dfrac{8.61}{143.5} = 0.06 \text{ mol}$

The ratio of moles of complex reacted to moles of $AgCl$ formed is:

$\text{Ratio} = \dfrac{0.02}{0.06} = \dfrac{1}{3} = 0.3333...$

This can be written as $33.33 \times 10^{-2}$.

Rounding to the nearest integer, we get $33$.

Answer: $33$

solution:
Ti(H2O)63+ ⇒Ti3+=3d1 4s0

⇒t2g1,0,0,eg0,0

CFSE =[-0.4nt2g+0.6neg] ∆0+n(p)

=[-0.4×1 ]20300

subject: Chemistry
q: Cobalt chloride when dissolved in water forms pink colored complex <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> which has octahedral geometry. This solution on treating with cone <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> forms deep blue complex, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> which has a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi></math> geometry. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi></math>, respectively, are
question: Cobalt chloride when dissolved in water forms pink colored complex <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi></math> which has octahedral geometry. This solution on treating with cone <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>HCl</mi></math> forms deep blue complex, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> which has a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi></math> geometry. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi></math>, respectively, are
solution:
Cobalt chloride when dissolved in water forms pink coloured isCoH2O6Cl2 . This is due to the electronic transition of an electron from t2g to eg energy level of CoH2O62+ complex. When excess of HCl is added to the solution CoH2O62+ is transformed into deep blue colour complex [CoCl4]2−.

△t= 49 △o

△t < △o

Tetrahedral complexes have smaller crystal field splitting than octahedral complexes.

CoCl2+6H2O⟶CoH2O6Cl2 

CoH2O62+ →Conc. HCl [CoCl4]2-

Hence, X=CoH2O62+, Y=CoCl42-, Z=Tetrahedral

solution:
$\begin{array}{|l|l|l|}

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements, one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A: The spin only magnetic moment value for Fe(CN)63- is 1.74 BM, whereas for [Fe(H2O)6]FeH2O63+ is 5.92 BM.

Reason B: In both complexes, Fe is present in +3 oxidation state.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements, one is labelled as Assertion A and the other is labelled as Reason R.

Assertion A: The spin only magnetic moment value for Fe(CN)63- is 1.74 BM, whereas for [Fe(H2O)6]FeH2O63+ is 5.92 BM.

Reason B: In both complexes, Fe is present in +3 oxidation state.

In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: A is false but R is true
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> is true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is false

Both <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> are true but <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>NOT</mi></math> the correct explanation of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi></math>
Both A and R are true andR is the correct explanation of A
solution:
[Fe(H2O)6]3+  has magnetic moment value of 5.92 BM because H2O  is a weak ligand and pairing will not occur. It involves sp3d2 hybridisation with five unpaired electrons (because magnetic moment is equal to 5.92 BM) and outer orbital complex is formed. Magnetic moment can be calculated by the formula μm=n(n+2) BM where, μm=magnetic moment, n=number of unpaired electron(s).

μm=1.74 BM, n=1μm=5.92 BM, n=5 

In [Fe(CN)6]3– complex CN− is stronger ligand than H2O according to spectrochemical series.  Hence, four electrons will pair itself. It involves d2sp3 hybridisation with one unpaired electron and inner orbital complex is formed and has a value of only 1.74 BM.

In Bothe the complexes the oxidation state of iron is +3.

subject: Chemistry
q:
In which of the following order the given complex ions are arranged correctly with respect to their decreasing spin only magnetic moment?
(i) FeF63-

(ii) CoNH363+

(iii) NiCl42-

(iv) CuNH342+
question:
In which of the following order the given complex ions are arranged correctly with respect to their decreasing spin only magnetic moment?
(i) FeF63-

(ii) CoNH363+

(iii) NiCl42-

(iv) CuNH342+
solution:
Complex
(i) FeF63-Fe3+⇒[Ar]3 d5, F- is WFL

n=5,  μ=35 B.M

(ii)CoNH363+⇒Co3+Ar3 d6,NH3 is SFL

n=0, μ=0 B.M

(iii) NiCl42-Ni2+⇒Ar3 d8,Cl-→WFL

n=2, μ=8 B.M

(iv) CuNH342+Cu2+⇒Ar3 d9,NH3→SFL

n=1, μ=3 B.M

Thus correct order of spin only magnetic moment is

(i)>(iii)>(iv)>(ii)

subject: Chemistry
q:
Which of the following are the example of double salt?

(A) FeSO4·NH42SO4·6H2O

(B) CuSO4·4NH3·H2O

(C) K2SO4·Al2SO43·24H2O

(D) Fe(CN)2·4KCN

Choose the correct answer.
question:
Which of the following are the example of double salt?

(A) FeSO4·NH42SO4·6H2O

(B) CuSO4·4NH3·H2O

(C) K2SO4·Al2SO43·24H2O

(D) Fe(CN)2·4KCN

Choose the correct answer.
solution:
A double salt is a salt that contains two or more different cations or anions.

(A) FeSO4 + NH42SO4 + 6H2O → FeSO4·NH42SO4·6H2O(Mohr's salt)

Mohr’s salt called double salt which contains more than one simple salt. It undergoes complete dissociation in aqueous solutions.

(B) CuSO4·4NH3·H2O

Ammonia solution in water gives a blue precipitate when it combines with a solution of copper salt. The pale blue precipitate of copper hydroxide dissolves in excess of ammonium hydroxide forming tetraamine copper[II] sulphate, an azure blue (deep blue) soluble complex salt.

(C) K2SO4·Al2SO43·24H2O (Potash alum)

It is a double salt. Basically, it is comprised of two simple salts potassium sulphate and aluminium sulphate. Potash alum will not be wet in its solid form because the water of crystallisation is not free.

(D) Fe(CN)2·4KCN

It is complex salt. It does not dissociate completely into individual ions.

So, the correct answer is A and C only.

subject: Chemistry
q:
The correct statements about metal carbonyls are
A. The metal-carbon bonds in metal carbonyls possess both $\sigma$ and $\pi$-character.
B. Due to synergic bonding interactions between metal and CO ligand, the metal-carbon bond becomes weak.
C. The metal-carbon $\sigma$ bond is formed by the donation of lone pair of electrons on the carbonyl carbon into a vacant orbital of metal.
D. The metal-carbon $\pi$ bond is formed by the donation of electrons from filled d-orbital of metal into vacant $\pi^*$ orbital of CO.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
The correct statements about metal carbonyls are
A. The metal-carbon bonds in metal carbonyls possess both $\sigma$ and $\pi$-character.
B. Due to synergic bonding interactions between metal and CO ligand, the metal-carbon bond becomes weak.
C. The metal-carbon $\sigma$ bond is formed by the donation of lone pair of electrons on the carbonyl carbon into a vacant orbital of metal.
D. The metal-carbon $\pi$ bond is formed by the donation of electrons from filled d-orbital of metal into vacant $\pi^*$ orbital of CO.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
Statement A is correct. The metal-carbon bond in metal carbonyls possesses both $\sigma$ and $\pi$ character due to synergic bonding.

Statement B is incorrect. The synergic bonding interaction between the metal and the CO ligand strengthens the metal-carbon bond.

Statement C is correct. The metal-carbon $\sigma$ bond is formed by the donation of a lone pair of electrons from the carbonyl carbon into a vacant orbital of the metal.

Statement D is correct. The metal-carbon $\pi$ bond is formed by the back-donation of electrons from a filled d-orbital of the metal into the vacant antibonding $\pi^{*}$ orbital of the CO ligand.

Therefore, statements A, C, and D are correct.

Answer: A, C and D Only

subject: Chemistry
q: The covalency and oxidation state respectively of boron in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced open="[" close="]"><msub><mi>BF</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>-</mo></msup></math>, are
question: The covalency and oxidation state respectively of boron in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced open="[" close="]"><msub><mi>BF</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></mfenced><mo>-</mo></msup></math>, are
solution:
Number of covalent bond formed by Boron is 4.

BF4- Covalency =4 

The oxidation state of an element represents the charge it would have if all the bonding electrons were assigned to the more electronegative atom in the bond.

Oxidation number of fluorine is -1

Then,

B + 4 x (-1) = -1B-4=-1B=+3

Oxidation state =+3 for Boron

q: An octahedral complex having molecular composition Co. $5 \mathrm{NH}_3. \mathrm{Cl}. \mathrm{SO}_4$ has two isomers A and B. The solution of A gives a white precipitate with $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$ solution and the solution of B gives white precipitate with $\mathrm{BaCl}_2$ solution. The type of isomerism exhibited by the complex is,
question: An octahedral complex having molecular composition Co. $5 \mathrm{NH}_3. \mathrm{Cl}. \mathrm{SO}_4$ has two isomers A and B. The solution of A gives a white precipitate with $\mathrm{AgNO}_3$ solution and the solution of B gives white precipitate with $\mathrm{BaCl}_2$ solution. The type of isomerism exhibited by the complex is,
solution:
(A) complex is $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_5\left(\mathrm{SO}_4\right)\right] \mathrm{Cl}$
(B) complex is $\left[\mathrm{Co}\left(\mathrm{NH}_3\right)_5 \mathrm{Cl}\right] \mathrm{SO}_4$
Both $(\mathrm{A})$ and $(\mathrm{B})$ are Ionisation isomers.

q:
$\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{PPh}_{3}\right)_{2} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}\right]$ is a paramagnetic complex. Identify the INCORRECT statements about this complex.
A. The complex exhibits geometrical isomerism.
B. The complex is white in colour.
C. The calculated spin-only magnetic moment of the complex is 2.84 BM .
D. The calculated CFSE (Crystal Field Stabilization Energy) of Ni in this complex is $-0.8 \Delta_{\mathrm{o}}$.
E. The geometrical arrangement of ligands in this complex is similar to that in $\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CO})_{4}$.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
$\left[\mathrm{Ni}\left(\mathrm{PPh}_{3}\right)_{2} \mathrm{Cl}_{2}\right]$ is a paramagnetic complex. Identify the INCORRECT statements about this complex.
A. The complex exhibits geometrical isomerism.
B. The complex is white in colour.
C. The calculated spin-only magnetic moment of the complex is 2.84 BM .
D. The calculated CFSE (Crystal Field Stabilization Energy) of Ni in this complex is $-0.8 \Delta_{\mathrm{o}}$.
E. The geometrical arrangement of ligands in this complex is similar to that in $\mathrm{Ni}(\mathrm{CO})_{4}$.
Choose the correct answer from the options given below :
solution:
In $[Ni(PPh_3)_2Cl_2]$, Nickel is in $+2$ oxidation state. $Ni^{2+}$ has a $3d^8$ electronic configuration.
The complex is given as paramagnetic. For a 4-coordinate $d^8$ system, paramagnetism implies a tetrahedral geometry ($sp^3$ hybridization) with two unpaired electrons.
Statement A: Tetrahedral complexes of the type $[MA_2B_2]$ do not exhibit geometrical isomerism because all positions are adjacent to each other. Thus, A is incorrect.
Statement B: Most $Ni(II)$ complexes are colored due to $d-d$ transitions. $[Ni(PPh_3)_2Cl_2]$ is a dark blue/green crystalline solid, not white. Thus, B is incorrect.
Statement C: With 2 unpaired electrons, the spin-only magnetic moment $\mu = \sqrt{n(n+2)} = \sqrt{2(2+2)} = \sqrt{8} \approx 2.83$ BM. Thus, C is correct.
Statement D: For a tetrahedral complex with $d^8$ configuration, the CFSE is calculated as $CFSE = [(-0.6 \times 4) + (0.4 \times 4)]\Delta_t = -0.8\Delta_t$. The statement mentions $-0.8\Delta_o$, which is incorrect as the complex is tetrahedral, not octahedral. Thus, D is incorrect.
Statement E: $Ni(CO)_4$ is a tetrahedral complex ($sp^3$ hybridization). Since $[Ni(PPh_3)_2Cl_2]$ is also tetrahedral, their geometrical arrangements are similar. Thus, E is correct.
The incorrect statements are A, B, and D.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Octahedral complexes of copper II undergo structural distortion (Jahn-Teller). Which one of the given copper II complexes will show the maximum structural distortion?
(en-ethylenediamine; H2N-CH2-CH2-NH2)
question:
Octahedral complexes of copper II undergo structural distortion (Jahn-Teller). Which one of the given copper II complexes will show the maximum structural distortion?
(en-ethylenediamine; H2N-CH2-CH2-NH2)
solution:
According to John teller any nonlinear molecular system in a degenerate electronic state will be unstable and will undergo some kind of distortion which will lower its symmetry and energy and split the degenerate state. In case of octahedral d9 configuration, the last electron may occupy either dz2 or dx2-y2orbitals of eg set. If it occupies dz2 orbital most of the electron density will be concentrated between the metal and the two ligands on the z axis. Thus there will be greater electrostatic repulsion associated with these ligands than with the other four on xy plane. The Jahn Teller effect is mostly observed in octahedral environments. The considerable distortions are usually observed in high spin d4, low spin d7 and d9 configuration

There is unsymmetrical filling of eg subset of Cu+2 ion, while there is symmetrical distribution in t2g set, if the complex has same ligand there will be equal repulsion which leads to symmetrical bond length along t2g, but due to uneven filling of electron in eg subset, either octahedral will be elongated or compressed.

Hence,CuH2O6SO4 maximum structural distortion

q: Aluminium chloride in acidified aqueous solution forms an ion having geometry
question: Aluminium chloride in acidified aqueous solution forms an ion having geometry
solution:
Aluminium Chloride in acidified aqueous solution forms octahedral Al(H2O)63+ ion.

AlCl3→Acidified aqueous solutionAlH2O63+

In this complex the 3d orbital of Al are involved and the hybridisation state of Al is sp3d2.

Hence, the answer is option A.

q: Sum of oxidation state (magnitude) and coordination number of cobalt in NaCobpyCl4 is
question: Sum of oxidation state (magnitude) and coordination number of cobalt in NaCobpyCl4 is
solution:
2,2′-Bipyridine is an organic compound, This colorless solid is an important isomer of the bipyridine family. It is a bidentate chelating ligand, forming complexes with many transition metals.

There is one bidentate ligand and four monodentate ligand attached with Co metal ion. So, Coordination no. will be =6

This bpy ligand has no net charge. and chloride ion has -1 charge. So, Oxidation state of metal ion will be=3

Their sub will be =6+3=9

Properties of the diamagnetic materials are: The diamagnetic materials have all the paired electrons, and none of the electrons is the valence, resulting in the absence of atomic dipoles in these materials. This happens because the overall magnetic moment of each atom in the compound cancels out.

NiCO4→ diamagnetic, sp3 hybridization, number of unpaired electrons =0

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements :
Statement I: $\quad\left[\mathrm{CoBr}_{4}\right]^{2-}$ ion will absorb light of lower energy than $\left[\mathrm{CoCl}_{4}\right]^{2-}$ ion.
Statement II : In $\left[\mathrm{CoI}_{4}\right]^{2-}$ ion, the energy separation between the two set of d-orbitals is more than $\left[\mathrm{CoCl}_{4}\right]^{2-}$ ion.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
question:
Given below are two statements :
Statement I: $\quad\left[\mathrm{CoBr}_{4}\right]^{2-}$ ion will absorb light of lower energy than $\left[\mathrm{CoCl}_{4}\right]^{2-}$ ion.
Statement II : In $\left[\mathrm{CoI}_{4}\right]^{2-}$ ion, the energy separation between the two set of d-orbitals is more than $\left[\mathrm{CoCl}_{4}\right]^{2-}$ ion.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below :
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
solution:
Crystal field theory explains d-d transitions in complexes based on ligand field strength.
Statement I: Ligand field strength order is I⁻ < Br⁻ < Cl⁻ < F⁻. Since Br⁻ is weaker than Cl⁻, [CoBr₄]²⁻ has smaller crystal field splitting (Δ) than [CoCl₄]²⁻. The d-d absorption energy equals the splitting energy, so [CoBr₄]²⁻ absorbs lower energy light. TRUE
Statement II: I⁻ is a much weaker field ligand than Cl⁻. Therefore, [CoI₄]²⁻ has smaller crystal field splitting than [CoCl₄]²⁻, not larger. The energy separation in [CoI₄]²⁻ is less than in [CoCl₄]²⁻. FALSE
Statement I is true but Statement II is false.
